headline: Long-running agent infrastructure, persistent memory, and self-improving skill libraries led the month while quantization and Rectified Flow broke out on the technique side.
body: The dominant theme is durable, long-running agents: Google Cloud Tech laid out event-driven dormancy, checkpointing, and separated evaluation for multi-day workflows, while subagents and content moderation held leading positions. Persistent memory and self-improving skill libraries surfaced repeatedly, with Hermes maintaining unified memory across Claude sessions and the Hermes Agent framework adding cron scheduling and self-written skills. Anthropic extended enterprise-managed auth for MCP connectors and shipped Artifacts in Claude Code, while Antigravity 2.0 pushed agent skill encoding and asynchronous task offloading on enterprise codebases. On the technique side quantization and Rectified Flow broke out, and Distillation, latent caching, and delegation chains entered as new.

cited_items.title: Hermes Agent Tutorial: How to Setup & Use Open Source AI Agent for Beginners
3 patterns to build long-running AI agents
Artifacts in Claude Code: share your work as it happens
7 tips for using Antigravity 2.0 on enterprise codebases, coding phase
